frameworks.name: dynamic circuit reconfiguration
frameworks.one_line: local cortical circuits transiently rewire to route sensory input to context-appropriate motor output

frameworks.name: mixed selectivity / conjunctive coding
frameworks.one_line: contingency neurons jointly code context and choice to implement flexible stimulus-response mapping

frameworks.name: abstraction over context
frameworks.one_line: alternative in which ALM would represent same-mapping contexts via a shared abstract code

frameworks.name: population coding in premotor cortex
frameworks.one_line: ALM as a site for sensorimotor decision representations

core_question: How does anterior lateral motor cortex implement context-dependent stimulus-response contingencies during flexible decision-making?
key_assumptions: two-photon imaging samples decision-relevant ALM populations
optogenetic silencing causally probes ALM's role per task epoch
context and test odors are drawn from separable odor sets
manipulations: context odor identity preceding the test odor
test odor identity paired with directional lick contingency
optogenetic silencing of ALM during context vs delay vs choice periods
key_findings: distinct ALM populations encode context, test odors, and choice
silencing ALM during context and delay impairs contingent performance
same-mapping context odors are represented by separate populations but converge at choice-selective neurons
a subpopulation of choice-selective neurons shows dual context-and-choice selectivity
interpretations: ALM configures the appropriate stimulus-response contingency rather than abstracting over context
flexible behavior arises from dynamic reconfiguration of local circuits forming contingency neurons
